reasoning: The men's 800m freestyle world record of 7:32.12 was set by Zhang Lin in 2009 during the 'super-suit' era. These records are notoriously difficult to break. With the conclusion of the World Championships, there are typically no major international long-course (50m) meets between September and February (this period is dominated by short-course/25m competitions where long-course records cannot be set). The base rate of a 16-year-old record being broken during the off-season is exceptionally low. Therefore, the probability that Zhang Lin will still hold the record is extremely high across all resolution dates. Probabilities are capped at 0.95 and tapered slightly over time to reflect increasing uncertainty over longer horizons as per instructions.
